title: Post-tonsillectomy follow-up

red_flags.text: Supports recording clinician-completed postoperative education, pain and hydration context, bleeding outcomes, monitoring, and follow-up. Medicines, doses, admission, discharge, and treatment instructions are excluded.
red_flags.rationale: The exact paediatric postoperative section supports interval change, pain and functional impact, hydration or bleeding context through associated symptoms and relevant negatives, vital signs, general appearance, ENT examination, clinician concern, entered plan, safety net, and follow-up without giving medication, dose, admission, discharge, or treatment instructions.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports recording clinician-completed postoperative education, pain and hydration context, bleeding outcomes, monitoring, and follow-up. Medicines, doses, admission, discharge, and treatment instructions are excluded.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Tonsillectomy in Children (Update)

exact_section.heading: Key action statements 9, 12, and 15 — pain education, monitoring context, and bleeding outcomes
exact_section.locator: official guideline key action statements 9, 12, and 15

source_limitations: Age, surgery date and indication, pain, intake, hydration, vomiting, fever, bleeding, airway, sleep, examination, medicines, doses, pathology, admission, discharge, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
